observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer to facilitate the submission of the updated list of inventory of insurable properties with the GSIS so that the necessary insurance coverage for all the assets can be applied with the GSIS General Insurance Fund.

We recommend that management should secure Permit to Purchase from the National Telecommunication Commission and a Dealer’s Permit of the supplier issued also by the NTC to consider the transaction of purchasing Radio Handheld Transceivers, Systems and Accessories a valid and legitimate expenditure. Such supporting documents are necessary to prevent transaction from being questioned in audit.
Further, it is recommended that all signing authorities in the management should at all times exercise their respective functions and be cautious in checking the appropriateness and completeness of all supporting documents on claims against the municipality for payment as one of the requirements of sound and strong internal control system.

We recommend that the Bids and Awards Committee prepare and submit the Procurement Monitoring Report for the 1st semester of CY 2017 to GPPB website on July 14, 2017; submit the CY 2018 Annual Procurement Plan-Common-use Supplies and Equipment to DBM on November 15, 2017 and prepare the APCPI for CY 2016 during the 1st quarter of CY 2017. The same shall be done on each ensuing semesters and years thereafter.

We are recommending the management to instruct the GSO and the Accountant to comply strictly with the provisions of Sections 114 and 121 of the Manual on NGAS for the proper recording of purchases and utilization of supplies and drugs and medicines and prepare the necessary adjusting entries to correct affected accounts.

We recommend to:
Require coordination between the Planning, Engineering, Treasury, Accounting, DSWD, DILG, and other concerned divisions/offices to fast track the implementation of BUB projects especially so that these projects are intended to provide benefits to the municipality’s grassroots population.
Require the BUB focal person to regularly monitor the status of proposed BUB projects as to funding and implementation.

Direct the General Service Officer to document the application for disposal of unserviceable property to the COA Auditor supported by the forms pursuant to the Handbook on Property and Supply Management System, and Section 79 of PD No. 1445.

Avoid the practice of procurement through reimbursement system and adopt the benefits of RA 9184 in pursuance to the declared policy of the State to promote the ideals of good governance of the LGU.
Instruct members of the Bids and Awards Committee to perform its mandated functions in accordance with the provisions of the Government Reform Act for the advantage of the LGU and paving avenue to equal opportunity among qualified and eligible bidders/suppliers in participating public bidding.

We recommend that the municipality ensure strict compliance to the provisions of Republic Act 9003 in proper segregation, collection, transport, storage, treatment and disposal of solid waste. Ensure the implementation of segregation at source of solid waste, enforcement of “no segregation, no collection’ policy, creation of Materials Recovery Facility (MRF) in every barangay, and the development of disposal facility in the municipality.

We recommend that management effect the transfer of its Public Infrastructure accounts to the Property, Plant and Equipment accounts of the LGU pursuant to the Philippine Public Sector Accounting Standards and COA Circular No. 2016-004 dated September 30, 2016.
We also recommend that the Local Chief Executive direct the following department heads for the compliance with the requirements of the COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated November 23, 2015:
a. Municipal Accountant
1. Prepare the Journal Voucher to record the beginning balance of the local road 	network and its components in the general ledger and the Local Road Network 	Ledger Card (Annex D of the Circular), respectively;
2. Support the JV with the Registry of the Public Infrastructures for the Roads, working paper on the distribution of costs for the road components, and working paper on the determination of the depreciated replacement cost for road components with no available cost per registry;
Keep and maintain subsidiary records for roads and road components for every road network; and
4. Prepare a lapsing schedule for the computation of the depreciation for each component at the end of the year.
b. Municipal General Services Officer/Municipal Treasurer
1. Maintain a Local Road Inventory and Road Map; and
2. Keep a complete Local Road Network Property Card for all roads and its components. (Annex E of the Circular)
c. Municipal Engineer
Provide the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal General Services Officer/Municipal Treasurer with the complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects.

We recommend that in the succeeding fiscal years, the municipality shall include sufficient appropriation in the Annual Budget for Implementation of the programs of the Local Councils for the Protection of Children as required under RA 9344 and DILG Memorandum Circular 2012-120.

Recommendation:
We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to review all financial records
and reports in order to ascertain the validity of the account balances that should be
presented in the financial statements and ensure that reports are prepared invariably in
accordance with generally accepted accounting principles and Section 69 of COA
Circular No. 2004-008.

Recommendations:
We recommended that:
a) The Municipal Treasurer produce immediately the disbursement vouchers to
support the credits to the Cash in Bank account totaling P12,685,325.41, otherwise
the same shall be disallowed in audit pursuant to Section 4(6) of P.D. 1445;
b) The Municipal Mayor institute reforms in the treasury procedures to ensure that all
evidences of payment are adequately secured, and initiate recovery measures for
failure to produce evidence of payments made;
c) The Municipal Accountant:
c.1) furnish our office with copies of the demand letters sent to the Municipal
Treasurer specifically for unsubmitted vouchers that were included in the Report of
Checks Issued;
c.2) Exhaust all means to reconstruct the details/schedule of the Accounts
Receivable in the Trust Fund; and
c.3) Prepare a Journal Entry Voucher reclassifying the said amount from
Accounts Receivable to Due from Officers and Employees. This entry,
however, does not in any way mean the authorization to make similar entries
for similar occurrences in the future.

Recommendations:
We recommended that the:
a) Inventory Committee give priority to the conduct of the physical count guided by
the regulations prescribed under Section 490 of the GAAM, Volume I, reconcile the
results thereof with the accounting records, prepare the report for submission to
our office and pinpoint responsibility for missing properties;
b) Municipal Treasurer, as property custodian in the absence of a General Services
Officer, secure all copies of the ARE and update the filing thereof, ensuring that
these are principally considered especially during the separation of accountable
officers from the government service;
c) Municipal Accountant invariably require that the ARE be fully complied with, in
form and quantity, as an indispensable supporting document for purchases of
movable PPE;
d) Management submit the schedule of PPE additions and disposals from CYs 2014-
2016, complete with data on the property tag, ARE, accountable officer,
office/location of the equipment, and the actual DVs and ARE; otherwise, those
that cannot be accounted for shall be accordingly disallowed; and
e) Municipal Accountant attach the formatted and detailed schedule of PPE additions
and disposals to the yearly financial report of the LGU and regularly update it to
facilitate the conduct of the physical inventory and to pinpoint responsibility for
government properties.

Recommendation:
We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to exhaust all means to secure
the various registries, trace the PIs that were previously transferred to the various
registries, identify those that would compose the 25% requirement, prepare the
necessary Journal Entry Voucher to facilitate recognition thereof in the books and,
henceforth, ensure that the required rate of PI recognition is complied with every year-
end until all the PIs are recorded back in the books by the end of CY 2019.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the LPRAT, through the MPDC, intensify efforts to fast-track
the implementation of the BUB program for the LGU to achieve its target of reducing
poverty and likewise avert any repercussions that may result from its low
implementation rate by assigning technical personnel who will focus on BUB projects
and by closely monitoring progress thereon. We further recommended that the
Municipal Accountant maintain subsidiary ledgers for all trust receipts, complete with
details and reconciled with controlling accounts, in order to facilitate reporting on
actual fund utilization.

Recommendations:
We recommended that the:
a) Municipal Accountant:
a.1) fast-track the preparation of the BRS by ensuring that personnel assigned
thereto give utmost priority to its completion in order to effect timely
adjustments to the recorded balances and to pinpoint accountability for any
irregular bank items or unauthorized bank transactions;
a.2) establish the unreconciled amount at the beginning of CY 2016 and
simultaneously commence work on two (2) fronts: 1 – workback on CY 2015
and prior years until any reconciling item is fully accounted for; and, 2 –
work forward on CY 2016 up to the present without further delay, ensuring
that the established reconciling item shall never increase but only decrease as
work on the PY backlogs progress.
b) Municipal Mayor closely monitor the regular submission of the BRS to the Auditor
for verification, inexcusably for CY 2016 henceforth, and ensure immediate
settlement of accountabilities for any unauthorized reconciling items so
determined; and
c) Management accord utmost importance to this critical and indispensable tool in
order to minimize the risk of loss/misuse of depository funds and to avoid
probable sanctions for gross negligence in the performance of duties.

Recommendations:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a) Assign additional personnel to assist the Municipal Treasurer in posting
transactions and updating her cashbooks for Cash in Treasury and Cash in Bank
and to reconcile the cashbooks with the ledger balances for these accounts;
b) Require the Municipal Treasurer to update her cashbooks daily, exercise adequate
supervision over any personnel assigned to post the transactions, and ensure that
all cashbooks are properly safeguarded against loss or misuse;
c) Direct the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to
c.1) account for the differences noted between the ledger and cashbook balances,
and henceforth conduct a quarterly reconciliation or as often as necessary;
c.2) establish a clear cut-off of accountabilities for the current and former
Municipal Treasurers, and fully disclose the existence of any shortage.
d) Closely monitor the Municipal Treasurer’s compliance to the strict requirements
pertaining to the handling and custody of cash and depository accounts.

Recommendation:
We recommended that Management, spearheaded by the MPDC, give due priority to
the preparation of the required plans, initiate the construction of the MRF after
approval thereof and fully undertake measures to comply with the requirements to pass
the SGLG and gain access to the PCF and the incentives package which could provide
essential funding for the LGU’s programs in addressing the eventuality of a disaster.
We further recommended that Management fast-track the completion of the revised
ISWMP 2017-2026 so it can be approved at the earliest possible time and thus provide
effective guidance to the LGU in undertaking programs for the protection of the
environment.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipality identify, inventory and classify its insurable
properties, submit the report to the GSIS not later than October 31 of each year,
appropriate the necessary premiums in its annual budget under the General Fund or
the 5% LDRRMF, and insure the same with the GIF under the GSIS every year.

Recommendation:
We recommended that Management desist from charging to government funds
expenditures related to beauty pageants and exercise caution in the allocation and
utilization of government funds for reception and entertainment, ensuring that
regulations for the prevention of irregular and unnecessary expenditures are observed.
We further recommended the immediate refund of the payments made for the beauty
pageants as these are disallowed in audit.

We recommended that the fidelity bond of the concerned Disbursing Officer be increased to an amount sufficient to cover the corresponding increase in her cash accountability for a given period in accordance with the revised schedule provided by the Bureau of the Treasury under TC No. 02-2009.
observations.managementAction: 2015 Already increased ML bond of DO in May 2016. Maximum accountability is now P1million.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Sangguniang Bayan, upon recommendation of the Municipal Mayor, enact an ordinance institutionalizing the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Office and appropriate adequate funds necessary for its operation, including the staffing requirements in compliance with NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 2014-1.
observations.managementAction: 2015 Creation of ML position for LDRRMO already approved by the Sangguniang Panlalawigan.
observations.reason: Funding for the position will be included in the CY 2018 budget.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management discontinue deducting from the payroll any payment to the aforementioned private financing institution, except for loans availed by the employees prior to receipt of this new Audit Observation Memorandum until such loans shall have been fully paid and, henceforth, desist from payroll deductions; and that the Municipal Accountant submit a status report on the existing loans from DCDB for monitoring purposes.
observations.managementAction: 2015 Perfected ML contracts are observed. No new loans are allowed. Ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to prepare immediately the 2015 BRS in accordance with Section 74 of P.D. 1445 and submit the same to the Auditor for verification, and henceforth, regularly as required. We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant accord utmost importance to this critical and indispensable tool in order to minimize the risk of loss/misuse of depository funds and to avoid probable sanctions for gross negligence in the performance of duties.
observations.managementAction: 2015 Not implemented initially; Municipal Accountant assigned to work on the bank reconciliation. AAR on the bank reconciliation prepared.
observations.reason: BRS preparation of backlogs is ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LDRRM Officer be directed to ensure faithful compliance to COA Circular No. 2012-002 and submit the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF prepared by the Municipal Accountant on or before the 15th day after the end of each month.
observations.managementAction: 2015 The Budget Officer prepared the monthly utilization report but did not submit a copy to COA.
observations.reason: They were not aware of the requirement to submit the said copy to COA.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant fast-track the preparation of the PPSAS-compliant CY 2015 financial statements and submit them to our office at the soonest possible time considering that these are long overdue. We further recommended that, henceforth, Management closely monitor invariable compliance to the submission of monthly and quarterly financial reports with complete schedules in accordance with the requirement of pertinent laws and regulations.
observations.managementAction: 2015 PPSAS-compliant ML financial statements for CY 2014 and 2015 were already submitted to COA.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the necessary adjusting entries to recognize the inventory and PPE accounts and to review prior years’ transactions for similar omissions in order to maintain the reliability of the financial statements. We further recommended that the General Services Office prepare and issue the Acknowledgment Receipt for Equipment so as to provide adequate accountability for government properties.
observations.managementAction: 2015 Not implemented; Management to prepare register and account for items.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that: a) The Municipal Treasurer produce immediately the disbursement vouchers to support the credits to the Cash in Bank account totaling P12,685,325.41, otherwise the same shall be disallowed in audit pursuant to Section 4(6) of P.D. 1445; b) The Municipal Mayor institute reforms in the treasury procedures to ensure that all evidences of payment are adequately secured and duly reported; c) The Municipal Accountant issue a demand letter to the Municipal Treasurer once unsubmitted vouchers are included in the Report of Checks Issued and initiate recovery measures for failure to act after due notice; and d) The Municipal Accountant prepare a Journal Entry Voucher reclassifying the said amount from Accounts Receivable to Due from Officers and Employees.
observations.managementAction: 2015 Not implemented; demand letters sent. A logbook system has been installed to monitor movements of vouchers.
observations.reason: Considerable balance still remains outstanding as of December 31, 2016.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management instruct the Inventory Committee to plan and strategize the conduct of the physical count of properties every year, giving emphasis to accountability for such properties and the sanctions provided under Sections 122 and 127 of P.D. 1445; submit the schedule of PPE additions and disposals within the year 2014, complete with the property tag, Acknowledgment Receipt for Equipment, accountable officer, office/location of the equipment; otherwise, those that cannot be accounted for shall be accordingly disallowed.
observations.managementAction: 2015 Hire additional personnel to be trained to assist the Inventory Committee. ML position for inventory assigned in 2013.
observations.reason: Funding for the position will be included in the CY 2018 budget; Inventory Committee tasking and staffing issues remain.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management submit the required documents enumerated above and secure the Transfer Certificate of Title of the lot purchased as an indispensable requirement.
observations.managementAction: 2015 Transfer Certificate of Title in the name of the LGU was already registered with the Register of Deeds on March 13, 2016.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the BAC and TWG be properly supervised and be made accountable for lapses in the performance of their functions. The BAC should prepare the checklists and require conformity in fulfilling all the official requirements indicated therein in order to preserve the integrity of the bidding process as mandated under R.A. 9184. We further recommended that Management submit a written explanation why the performance security and/or required was a flat 10%, contrary to regulations, why the required warranty in the bid instruction allowed a 5% bank guarantee, contrary to regulations, and why there was no warranty actually secured from the winning bidder prior to payment.
observations.managementAction: 2014 The accounting department of the LGU-La Libertad acknowledged the need for the BAC, accounting personnel, TWG and others to attend to RA#9184 IRR and GPPB Pronouncements.
observations.reason: Documents submitted to the audit team still need to be evaluated.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management immediately effect the closure of the cash balance of the PNB Time Deposit and COMBO accounts, and transfer the same either to LBP or DBP, whichever offers the optimal value for the LGU, in compliance with Section 311 of R.A. 7160, DOF D.O. No. 27-05, COA Circular No. 92-382, and BTr TC No. 01-2013.
observations.managementAction: 2014 Closure of PNB Time Deposit account was implemented; no more deposits but only check issuances until the account is fully depleted.
observations.reason: Closure upon depletion of funds.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management set a time frame for the MSWMB to formulate the Integrated Solid Waste Management Plan in order to maximize the pervasive benefits forthwith considering the length of time that has lapsed since the effectivity of R.A. 9003, to which the Municipality has not complied, and to avoid possible administrative sanctions; and ensure that proper mechanisms, like budget and manpower, preferably a full-time Municipal Environment and Natural Resources Office/r, are in place once the ISWMP is approved.
observations.managementAction: 2014 ISWMP already expired in 2016. Current ISWMP for updating. No full time MENRO; only designate.
observations.reason: Data-base updating, preparation of waste characterization data for ISWMP updating.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management adopt measures to mitigate the effects of the current open dump site; concretize methods and procedures for the phaseout/eventual closure of the existing open dump site within a specific time frame; and request the Sangguniang Bayan to prioritize the allocation of funds for the establishment and operation of a sanitary landfill in accordance with Sections 40 and 41 of R.A. 9003.
observations.managementAction: 2014 LGU has allocated P1,000,000.00 for the purchase of lot for the controlled dump site or sanitary landfill. The LGU is still looking for the lot compliant with DENR Criteria.
observations.reason: Difficulty encountered for lot acquisition that is compliant with DENR Criteria.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the persons liable for the excess payment of the CY 2014 PEI immediately restitute the same to the government, in accordance with Section 11.0 of DBM BC No. 2014-3 and henceforth, Management should strictly observe the applicable regulations on the grant of any benefits/incentives.
observations.managementAction: 2014 Excess PEI fully paid.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality identify, inventory and classify its insurable properties, submit the report to the GSIS not later than October 31 of each year, appropriate the necessary premiums in its annual budget under the General Fund or the 5% LDRRMF, and insure the same with the Property Insurance Fund under the GSIS.
observations.managementAction: 2014 Management welcomed the recommendation and will bring this up with the Local Chief Executive.
observations.reason: The Inventory Committee was not able to conduct the physical count of properties nor did it prepare the inventory report of insurable properties.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management instruct the Local Finance Committee to process the immediate return of the unutilized PDAF funds of P72,846,900.30 to the National Treasury, through the Department of Finance, in compliance with the Supreme Court Decision; submit a written explanation why the said funds have not been returned despite the SC ruling and why disbursements were still made after such declaration; and submit a summary of disbursements from November 19, 2013 to December 31, 2014 with details.
observations.managementAction: 2014 A letter, dated October 15, 2015 has been sent to the COA Audit team for the planned action of the LGU to continue disbursing the said funds based on the DBM letter-advice concerning the PDAF funds on the LGU position.
observations.reason: The LGU did not return the PDAF to the national treasury since they insist on its legality; updated remaining balance as of December 31, 2016 is P47,592,040.52.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant issue demand letters to all incumbent and previous personnel for the liquidation of their outstanding cash advances, citing the sanctions which will be imposed on them, such as withholding of salaries, the initiation of administrative proceedings and the filing of appropriate malversation charges under the Revised Penal Code.
observations.managementAction: 2014 Demand letters sent. Quarterly cash advance reports prepared and actions taken.
observations.reason: Considerable balance still remains outstanding as of December 31, 2016; cash advance reports contain multiple errors and do not reconcile with ending balances of preceding quarter.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the BAC to prepare an APP from the respective PPMP submitted by the end-user units, which shall be the basis for procurement of the supplies requirements of the municipality; and require the BAC to plan and conduct procurement based on the approved APP in order that the procurement of goods can be done, as much as possible, through competitive bidding pursuant to Section 10 of R.A. 9184.
observations.managementAction: 2013 AAR prepared. Management implemented procedures.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer release immediately the full amount of P94,931.75 due the different barangays and strictly adhere to the prescribed period for releasing/remitting the barangays’ share of real property taxes, which is five (5) days after the end of each quarter.
observations.managementAction: 2013 The succeeding years are already prepared by MTO personnel.

observations.recommendation: It is recommended that Management record purchases of supplies to the respective inventory accounts, require the use of the RIS for issuance of supplies, prepare a weekly SSMI to be used as basis for recognition of the expense, as well as maintain perpetual inventory records to be reconciled periodically with the Municipal Treasurer’s records.
observations.managementAction: 2013 Implemented.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant facilitate the completion of the subject disbursements and submit the required supporting documents, otherwise the aforementioned transactions will be suspended in audit. Direct him, as well, not to approve transactions that lack complete documentation and to always adhere to Section 138 (f) of the GAAM, Volume I.
observations.managementAction: 2013 Subsequent disbursement vouchers are required to be supported and adequately signed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management formulate a GAD Plan and Budget in accordance with Section 37 of the IRR of R.A. 9170 and Joint Circular No. 2013-01 and submit the same to the Philippine Commission on Women for approval. Ensure that only those programs, projects and activities that address gender gaps and issues be considered and included therein.
observations.managementAction: 2013 GAD Plan and Budget for 2012 prepared as part of the annual budget.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer furnish the Municipal Accountant with the certified list of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year in accordance with Sec.20 of the NGAS for LGUs, Vol. I as basis for the preparation of the JEV setting up the RPT/SET Receivables at the beginning of the year.
observations.managementAction: 2012 We requested from the Municipal Assessor for the certified list of taxpayers.
observations.reason: As much as possible, we tried to avoid non-bonded personnel to perform collections; still ongoing due to new personnel assigned to do the job.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require only duly bonded and designated employees/staff perform collecting functions.
observations.managementAction: 2012 As much as possible, we tried to avoid non-bonded personnel to perform collections.
observations.reason: Still in the process of securing the bond for the assigned collectors.

observations.recommendation: For subsequent transactions, we recommended that copies of the perfected and/or approved contracts and purchase orders and the like as well as its related supporting documents be furnished to the Auditor as required. Adherence to COA Circular No. 2009-001 is enjoined.
observations.managementAction: 2012 Implemented.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to immediately remit the amount of withholding taxes particularly that of prior years pursuant to Section 4 (2) (a) of Revenue Regulation No. 6-2001 and Section 3.C (B) of Revenue Regulation No. 2-2006.
observations.managementAction: 2012 Implemented.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management require the Municipal Accountant to submit to the Office of the Auditor the documents enumerated, otherwise the aforementioned transactions will be suspended in audit. Direct him also not to pass transactions that lack complete documentation and to always adhere to Section 138 (f) of the GAAM Vol. 1.
observations.managementAction: 2011 Implemented.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management direct the Municipal Treasurer to intensify collection of real property taxes due from the different delinquent taxpayers and to apply the remedies provided under Sections 254 and 258 of the Local Government Code especially against those with delinquent accounts of more than two (2) years.
observations.managementAction: 2011 Implemented.
observations.reason: Per FS, balance has ballooned to P4,480,007.43.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management request the Prov’l. Appraisal Committee (PAC) to determine the value of the unrecorded lots. Also, direct the Municipal Assessor to process the titling of the lots owned by the LGU. Likewise, instruct the Municipal Accountant to review the land account and record those lots that are owned by the LGU. Moreover, the value of the lots which will be determined by the PAC should be recorded in the books.
observations.managementAction: 2010 The LGU initially applied for the Register of Deeds titling; the DENR-CENRO office cannot issue the titling of lots with special patent for LGUs because they are waiting for the Memorandum of Agreement (MOA) for special patent.
observations.reason: The DENR-CENRO office cannot issue the titling of lots with special patent for LGUs; waiting for Memorandum of Agreement (MOA).

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management direct the Municipal Treasurer to intensify collection of rentals due from the different delinquent stall owners by sending demand letters and by applying the remedies that are provided for under Section 5B.06 of its Revenue Ordinance.
observations.managementAction: 2010 Implemented.

observations.recommendation: Require the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to maintain complete property cards and PPE ledger cards, respectively, in compliance with Section 114 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs.
observations.managementAction: 2007 Implemented.
